title: Arthratherum vestitum
intro: Arthratherum vestitum, also known as White Fescue, is a perennial grass species that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Arthratherum vestitum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Arthratherum vestitum is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being used in the form of a tincture, to alleviate respiratory symptoms and promote overall well-being.
